# US Congress criticizes Pete Hegseth over unapproved Iran war launch

## Overview
Pete Hegseth faced sharp criticism in Congress for launching the Iran conflict without approval. Democrats condemned the war's justification and its estimated $25 billion cost.[1]

## Details
Criticism centered on lack of congressional authorization for military action against Iran. Donald Trump rejected Iran’s proposal on the Strait of Hormuz, stalling diplomacy. Tehran remains seeking international support amid the impasse. The event highlights escalating tensions in the ongoing US-Iran conflict. Hegseth's testimony drew bipartisan scrutiny on war costs and strategy.[1]

**Location**: Washington, United States

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @cnni, which covers CNN International world breaking news conflicts. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following CNN International world breaking news conflicts will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
